data.content:
[Claude 2026-05-08] # Implementation Review — APPROVE

## Verdict
Approve. Implementation matches the plan and meets all acceptance criteria.

## Evidence
1. Branch feature/knots-242a-e2e-boundary at 493ab6ca contains the work
   described in the plan note: ~821 insertions across src/, skills/, docs,
   and tests.
2. Acceptance criteria coverage:
   - AC1 (machine-readable signal): JSON output adds 'e2e' bool and
     'workflow_boundary_kind' (single_action | e2e_continuation). The
     prompt body also carries 'kind: `single_action`' or
     'kind: `e2e_continuation`' lines (src/prompt.rs).
   - AC2 (continue after kno next): e2e boundary text explicitly tells
     the agent to re-claim with 'kno claim --e2e <id>' after kno next,
     and to continue until SHIPPED/BLOCKED/DEFERRED.
   - AC3 (default one-action preserved): default boundary text still
     contains 'Complete exactly one workflow action, then stop.';
     verified by render_single_action_boundary_when_e2e_false and
     claim_without_e2e_flag_keeps_single_action_workflow_boundary.
   - AC4 (terminal-state authorization gated): e2e boundary contains
     'Terminal-state movement is authorized for this e2e run.'; default
     boundary makes no such claim.
   - AC5 (docs): README.md adds 'Workflow boundary kinds' section and
     verbatim user-facing override wording. AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md add
     a 'Claim Boundary Precedence' section. skills/knots_e2e.md adds
     'Invocation precedence' and switches all examples to '--e2e'.
3. Constraint respected: default claim safety is unchanged. ClaimArgs/
   PollArgs default e2e to false; ClaimOperation/PollClaimOperation use
   serde default; render_workflow_boundary branches on e2e and only the
   e2e branch authorizes continuation.
4. Test coverage: prompt_tests.rs adds 6 tests for both branches and
   JSON fields; tests_inline.rs verifies completion_command does not
   emit '--e2e' on kno next; tests/cli_claim_e2e.rs adds end-to-end
   integration tests for both 'kno claim --e2e <id>' and default
   'kno claim <id>' paths.
5. Sanity gates: 'make sanity' on the feature branch ran 1132 tests
   (0 failures) and reported 95.25% coverage, above the 95% threshold.
6. No security issues or regressions identified in the diff.

## Completion
Advancing to shipment via 'kno next'.
